title: Shiak
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
{{Cleanup}}
{{Quote|Shiak is 'stabbing.' Jedi tradition holds that shiak is an honorable method of inflicting serious injury because it causes the least visible injury to the opponent's body.|[[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]]|<ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
[[File:AgenKolarDies-ROTS.jpg|thumb|right|260px|[[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]] is slain through [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' use of ''shiak'']]
In [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], '''''shiak''''' was the act of stabbing an opponent and was considered relatively honorable by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Jedi usually stabbed in the leg or arm, while [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] preferred stabbing opponents in the head, the chest or the stomach, often guaranteeing certain death to the unfortunate victim.

==History==
[[Jedi Consular]] [[Raana Tey]] received a ''shiak'' strike from [[Shel Jelavan]], though it did not immediately end her life.<ref name="KOTOR">[[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]]</ref>
[[File:Sith_penetrated.png|thumb|right|260px|Kao Cen Darach injuring Vindican]]
''Shiak'' was featured several times during the duel on board [[Korriban's orbital security station]] between [[Sith Inquisitor]] [[Darth Vindican|Vindican]], his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] [[Darth Malgus|Malgus]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kao Cen Darach]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Satele Shan]]. Vindican attempted to impale Darach in the face, but the Zabrak Jedi easily dodged the Inquisitor's strike. Later while single-handedly fighting the two Sith, Darach incapacitated Vindican by activating his apprentice's second blade on her saberstaff through the Sith's abdomen.<ref name="Return">{{TORweb|url=info/media/trailers/return|text="Return"|int=Return (cinematic trailer)}}</ref>

The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Scientist/Legends|Scientist]] [[Demagol]] and his [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]] daughter, [[Chantique]], both received a ''shiak'' strike from [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] [[Exar Kun's lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]].<ref name="KotOR 50">[[Knights of the Old Republic 50|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 50]]</ref> This was a result of the latter attempting to attack the former, who grabbed the lightsaber to defend himself but mistakenly activated both ends of it, impaling and killing them both.

Jedi Master [[Meetra Surik]] received a killing blow from behind from [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Scourge (Sith)|Scourge]], when he unexpectedly turned on his allies during their [[attack on the Sith Emperor]].<ref name="Revan">''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]''</ref>

Jedi Master [[Ven Zallow]] was defeated by Darth Malgus' ''shiak'' during the [[Sacking of Coruscant]], killing him.<ref name="Deceived">{{TORweb|url=info/media/trailers/deceived-cinematic-trailer|text=Deceived Cinematic Trailer|int=Deceived}}</ref>

Jedi Master [[Orgus Din]] was impaled by [[Darth Angral]] after trying to assault Angral's cruiser.<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref>
[[File:QuiGonJinnDeath-TPM.png|thumb|left|250px|[[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] is killed by a ''shiak'' strike from [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]].]]

During a [[Duel on Tython|skirmish]] on [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]] applied ''shiak'' against the [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] Jedi Master [[Raskta Lsu]] from behind, ending the Jedi's life.<ref name="DBROT">''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''</ref> In that same battle, [[Johun Othone]] died at Zannah's hands in a similar manner when she drove her lightsaber through his chest after he was injured by [[Darth Bane/Legends|Zannah's master]].<ref name="DBROT" />

[[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] used ''shiak'' when he stabbed Jedi Master [[Siolo Ur Manka]].<ref name="Nameless">{{Tales|10|Nameless (comic story)}}</ref> He used it again on [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] during their [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|duel]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="SWTPM">{{Film|I}}</ref>. Maul performed ''shiak'' again on [[Finn Ertay/Legends|Finn Ertay]] from behind during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] when the two [[Duel at unidentified Outer Rim spaceport/Legends|dueled]] and again on [[Sabo/Legends|Sabo]] over [[Florrum/Legends|Florrum]].<ref name="Rev">{{TCW|Revival (episode)}}</ref> The Sith Lord executed [[Satine Kryze/Legends|Satine Kryze]] with a ''shiak'' strike by pulling her onto the [[Darksaber/Legends|Darksaber]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="Lawless">{{TCW|The Lawless}}</ref>

''Shiak'' was used by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. He killed a [[T'surr (species)|T'surr]] slave trader named [[Krayn]] by stabbing him through the chest.<ref name="JQ">''[[Jedi Quest: Path to Truth]]''</ref> He also applied this technique to [[Tal Merrik/Legends|Tal Merrik]] from behind, a turncoat [[Senator/Legends|senator]] working for [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] who was trying to hold [[Satine Kryze/Legends|Satine Kryze]] hostage.<ref name="VOT">{{TCW|Voyage of Temptation}}</ref> The [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] also applied this technique to kill the powerful Force-wielder known as the [[Son/Legends|Son]] from behind and [[Father (Mortis)/Legends|his Father]]<ref name="Ghosts of Mortis">{{TCW|Ghosts of Mortis}}</ref> and again on several [[Zygerrian/Legends|Zygerrians]] during the [[Battle of Kadavo/Legends|Battle of Kadavo]].<ref name="EFK">{{TCW|Escape from Kadavo}}</ref>

[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] attempted to use ''shiak'' on [[Durge/Legends|Durge]] during the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]], but failed due to the durable nature of [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Durge's species]].<ref name="Star Wars: Clone Wars">{{CW|8}}</ref> He also used the technique against [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] on [[Christophsis/Legends|Christophsis]], in addition to numerous other battles during the Clone Wars.<ref name="TCW film">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]]</ref> On two occasions, he used ''sai cha'' in conjunction with ''shiak'' on two battle droids, one aboard the ''Saak'ak'' and one during the mission to rescue [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell]] from the Citadel.

[[File:KrellVsClones-CoK.png|thumb|right|280px|Fallen Jedi Pong Krell uses ''shiak'' against clone troopers]]
''Shiak'' was used by [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] when she impaled Jedi Master [[Fay]] from behind.<ref name="Star Wars Republic 53: Blast Radius">[[Republic 53|''Republic'' 53]]</ref> She used it again on Senate Commando [[Faro Argyus/Legends|Faro Argyus]] when he tried to take all the credit for the [[Rescue on the Tranquility/Legends|rescue]] of [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]].<ref name="Cloak of Darkness">{{TCW|Cloak of Darkness}}</ref> She also applied this technique with a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Choke]] to Clone Recon Commando [[Colt/Legends|Colt]] during the [[Battle of Kamino/Legends|Battle of Kamino]]<ref name="ARC Troopers">{{TCW|ARC Troopers (episode)}}</ref> and again on the Bounty Hunter [[Oked/Legends|Oked]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] after [[Battle of Dathomir/Legends|Battle of Dathomir]].<ref name="Bounty">{{TCW|Bounty (episode)}}</ref>

[[Warden/Legends|Warden]] [[Osi Sobeck/Legends|Osi Sobeck]] received a ''shiak'' strike from behind by Jedi Padawan [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]], when he attempted to assassinate [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] during the [[Battle of Lola Sayu/Legends|Battle of Lola Sayu]].<ref name="Citadel Rescue">{{TCW|Citadel Rescue}}</ref>

[[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] applied ''shiak'' to kill his former friend [[Lorian Nod]], who tried to prevent his escape on [[Null/Legends|Null]].<ref name="Legacy of the Jedi">''[[Legacy of the Jedi]]''</ref>. He also tried to use ''shiak'' on Asajj Ventress during the [[Showdown at Toydaria/Legends|Showdown at Toydaria]], but she redirected it with the Force and he instead stabbed a pipe. He later impaled [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Minister]] [[Rish Loo/Legends|Rish Loo]] during the [[Skirmish on Naboo/Legends|Skirmish on Naboo]].<ref name="Shadow Warrior">{{TCW|Shadow Warrior}}</ref> He used ''shiak'' on the [[Pyke Syndicate/Legends|Pike Syndicate's]] Minister [[Lom Pyke/Legends|Lom Pyke]] during [[Mission to Oba Diah (Clone Wars)/Legends|Mission to Oba Diah]].

[[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] "rewarded" [[Gha Nachkt/Legends|Gha Nachkt's]] finding of Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker's astromech droid [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and cracking his never-been-erased memory banks by impaling him through the back.<ref name="Duel of the Droids">{{TCW|Duel of the Droids}}</ref> He also used it on the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]] [[Daka/Legends|Daka]] during the [[Battle of Dathomir/Legends|Battle of Dathomir]].<ref name="Massacre">{{TCW|Massacre}}</ref> During the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], he used it to devastating effect several times slaying Jedi opponents [[Roron Corobb/Legends|Roron Corobb]], [[B'ink Utrila]], [[Pablo-Jill/Legends|Pablo-Jill]], and [[L'lacielo Sageon]].<ref name="Labyrinth">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref>

[[File:StarKillerShiak.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Starkiller is impaled by his Master, Darth Vader.]]
[[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]] used ''shiak'' on the mind controlled [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Trap/Legends|Trap]] when he tried to infect her with a [[Brain worm/Legends|brain worm]].<ref name="Brain Invaders">{{TCW|Brain Invaders}}</ref>

Jedi Master [[Pong Krell/Legends|Pong Krell]] used ''shiak'' to slay several clone troopers on [[Umbara/Legends|Umbara]] after he revealed his fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="CoK">{{TCW|Carnage of Krell}}</ref>

The [[Ming Po/Legends|Ming Po]] [[Tryla/Legends|Tryla]] received a ''shiak'' strike from the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch's]] leader, [[Secret Mandalore]] [[Pre Vizsla/Legends|Pre Vizsla]], for attempting to kill Ming Po's Grandfather [[Chief/Legends|Chieftain]] [[Pieter/Legends|Pieter]] during the [[Battle on Carlac/Legends|first skirmish]] on [[Carlac/Legends|Carlac]].<ref name="AFIN">{{TCW|A Friend in Need}}</ref>

[[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Nightbrothers/Legends|Nightbrother]] [[Savage Opress/Legends|Savage Opress]] used ''shiak'' on Jedi Master [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] on [[Florrum/Legends|Florrum]] after goring her with his horns.

[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] used this maneuver to kill Nightbrother [[Savage Opress/Legends|Savage Opress]] during their [[Battle of Mandalore/Legends|duel]] on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Showdown on Coruscant|and]] on Jedi Master [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]] shortly before fatally slashing [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]], when a team of Jedi Masters went to arrest him.<ref name="SWROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref>

During and after the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], Darth Vader used ''shiak'' multiple times. During Vader's [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|attack]] on the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], his first Jedi victim was Gatekeeper [[Jurokk]] by igniting his lightsaber through Jurokk's head. He also murdered [[Chief Librarian/Legends|Chief Librarian]] [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]] by lifting her into the air and impaling her through the chest. Shortly afterwards, in the hidden passageways underneath the Temple, he killed [[Sha Koon]] in a similar manner. [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] used ''shiak'' to slay several clone troopers in the Jedi Temple. An unidentified clone trooper of the 501st legion was killed in this manner as well when the diminutive Master hurled his saber at him and it impaled him through the chest. Later that year, when he assailed a [[Conclave on Kessel|secret meeting of surviving Jedi]], the first to die by his blade was the Jedi Knight [[Sia-Lan Wezz]], having been impaled by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]]; he then [[Cho mai/Legends|sliced off her hand]] as she fell dead.<ref name="Purge">[[Purge (comic book)|''Purge'']]</ref> Vader also ended the life of Jedi Master [[Hylon]] when he impaled him through the chest atop [[Mount Dijandi]] on [[Otavon XII]]. Jedi Padawan [[Drake Lo'gaan/Legends|Drake Lo'gaan]] was also stabbed through the chest by Darth Vader, though unlike Hylon, Lo'gaan survived the blow. Also, shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Vader again applied ''shiak'' in a battle against a [[hyenax]] pack on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Vaal/Legends|Vaal]], where he stabbed one of the attacking animals through its skull.<ref name="Savage Heart">[[Empire 14|''Empire'' 14]]</ref>

The simulacrum of [[Darth Phobos]] was defeated by [[Galen Marek]] stabbing her in the back. Marek himself was impaled by Darth Vader on the bridge of the [[Executor/Legends|Executor]] when Darth Sidious "discovered" him and was then flung into space. He would survive the betrayal, though he spent six months on the ''[[Empirical]]'' recovering.<ref name="TFU">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

[[File:Brutality-CotF.png|thumb|250px|right|A Zabrak Dark Jedi uses ''shiak'' against a Rebel trooper.]]
During their [[Battle of Mustafar (Galactic Civil War)|showdown]] on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], [[X1]] threw his lightsaber at his brother, [[X2]]. However, X2 dodged, and X1's blade ran him through instead, causing him to stagger off a nearby cliff to his death.

[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] killed his former student [[Nelani Dinn]] with ''shiak'' after immobilizing her with ''[[Cho mok/Legends|cho mok]]'' and ''[[Shiim/Legends|shiim]]'' strikes during their [[Duel at The Home|mission]] at [[The Home]] near [[Bimmiel/Legends|Bimmiel]].<ref name="Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref>

Darth Caedus skewered [[Battlemaster/Legends|Jedi Battlemaster]] [[Kyle Katarn]] with this technique, though Katarn would survive [[Undercity Ambush|the encounter]].<ref name="Fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>

''Shiak'' was used by [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] to defeat [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]], killing him by running her lightsaber through his neck during the [[Battle of Ebaq]]. [[41 ABY|Thirteen years later]], she used it again on her brother, Jacen Solo, by then Darth Caedus, impaling him through the abdomen when she ambushed him during [[Battle of Uroro Station|their final duel]]. Caedus survived the blow, however.<ref name="Invincible">''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]''</ref>

[[Relin Druur]] [[Telekinesis/Legends|lifted]] [[Saes Rrogon]] with the Force and stabbed his former Padawan with his own lightsaber, killing him instantly.<ref name="Crosscurrent">''[[Crosscurrent]]''</ref>

[[File:DeathofWyyrlok.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Krayt kills [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] with ''shiak''.]]
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] accidentally killed [[Dyon Stadd]] by stabbing him in the chest when the dark side being [[Abeloth]] disguised herself as him.<ref name="Allies">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Allies]]''</ref>

[[Darth Nihl]] used this technique against Zabrak Jedi Master [[Wolf Sazen]] during the [[Massacre at Ossus]], though Sazen would survive the battle.<ref name="SWL 1">[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]]</ref>

Imperial Knight [[Elke Vetter]] received a ''shiak'' strike to her right leg from [[Darth Talon]].<ref name="SWL 3">[[Legacy (2006) 3|''Legacy'' (2006) 3]]</ref> Talon herself was later stabbed through the stomach by [[Cade Skywalker]].<ref name="SWL 18">[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]]</ref>

''Shiak'' was used by [[Darth Krayt]], when he impaled the [[Imperial Knight]] [[Mohrgan Fel]] through the chest during his [[Attack on Emperor Fel|raid]] on the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]].<ref name="SWL 1" /><ref name="SWL 8">[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]]</ref> He used it again on his former adviser, [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]], during a [[Assault on the Temple of the Sith|battle]] for control of the [[One Sith]].<ref name="SWL—War 1">[[Legacy — War 1|''Legacy — War'' 1]]</ref> Krayt himself was a victim of two ''shiak'' strikes. The first time this happened, he was stabbed through his upper back by Imperial Knight [[Azlyn Rae]] when she and her allies [[Battle on Had Abbadon|attempted to assassinate him]], though it failed to kill him.<ref name="SWL 31">[[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]]</ref> Later, he was fatally impaled through the chest by Cade Skywalker during their [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|final showdown]].<ref name="SWL—War 6">[[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy — War'' 6]]</ref>

Imperial Knight [[Antares Draco]] skewered Emperor [[Roan Fel]] from behind when he attempted to release a sample of [[Omega Red]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], fulfilling his oath to kill his Emperor if he fell to the dark side and could not be redeemed.<ref name="SWL—War 6" />

==Behind the scenes==
Two instances of [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] was being killed with a ''shiak'' strike were made for [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']], but both were [[Content cut from Star Wars#Episode III - ''Revenge of the Sith''|cut]]. One involves General Grievous having kidnapped and brought Ti aboard the ''[[Invisible Hand/Legends|Invisible Hand]]''. Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi proceed to encounter them, but before they can do anything, Grievous, standing behind Ti, holds a lightsaber behind her heart and ignites it, killing her. The second scene features Ti meditating in a chamber during Order 66. Skywalker enters, prompting Ti to acknowledge him, though he stays silent, walks behind Ti, and activates his lightsaber through her chest. Both of these are considered to be non-canon, as ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' depicts her [[Mission to Felucia (Great Jedi Purge)|death]] to be years later, at the hands of [[Galen Marek]] on [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]].<ref name="TFU" />

In the video game ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'', the player, if he or she stays on the light side, can stab any opponent in the game. However, if a player chooses to turn to the dark side, Jaden Korr kills Rosh Penin by ''shiak''. After Jaden leaves, Kyle enters and finds Rosh who is severely injured, who says he was sorry that both of Kyle's students had failed him. Before Rosh dies, Kyle assures him that he had become a Jedi.
